commit.message:
fix: repair invite flow — route through auth callback and restore middleware

The invite/recovery redirectTo URL pointed directly to /reset-password,
but with PKCE flow Supabase sends a ?code= param that the reset-password
page doesn't handle.  Route through /api/auth/callback instead, which
exchanges the code for a session then forwards to /reset-password.

Also:
- Restore middleware.ts (was renamed to proxy.ts which Next.js ignores)
- Send Supabase invite email for new users (generateLink only creates
  a link, it does not send an email)
- Show invite_expired error banner on the login page when callback fails

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extract venue eligibility thresholds and add fitness review filter (#100)

* Extract venue eligibility thresholds and add fitness review filter

- Add VENUE_MIN_REVIEWS and VENUE_MIN_RATING constants to scoring_config.py
  (coffee_social: 30/4.0, provisioning: 20/3.5, fitness: 10/3.5)
- Replace hardcoded filters in score_third_place_access and
  score_provisioning_access with the new constants (no behavioral change)
- Add eligibility filter to score_fitness_access — venues below the
  review/rating thresholds are excluded from scoring but still shown
  in neighborhood cards
- When no fitness venues pass the threshold, return points=None with
  suppressed_reason so the template shows "—" instead of a numeric score
- Add suppressed_reason field to Tier2Score dataclass (Optional[str])
- Guard aggregation in evaluate_property() to skip None-scored dimensions
- Serialize suppressed_reason through app.py → dimension_summaries → template
- Template shows em-dash and suppressed bar for suppressed dimensions,
  still renders venue cards below

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FcbHUqQcYoneuKh7Cr3ahN

* Add quality ceiling to coffee/social scoring based on scene diversity

Phase 2: The walk-time score alone produces 10/10 when any qualifying
venue is within 15 min, regardless of the quality of the social scene.

Add a quality-adjusted ceiling computed from two signals:

1. Category diversity — map Google Places types to social buckets
   (coffee, bakery, bar/nightlife, restaurant, other). Distinct bucket
   count sets the ceiling: 1→5, 2→7, 3→9, 4+→10.

2. Review depth — median review count across qualifying venues adjusts
   the ceiling ±1 (below 50 → -1, above 200 → +1).

Final score = min(walk_time_score, quality_ceiling).

New constants in scoring_config.py:
  THIRD_PLACE_CATEGORY_CEILINGS, THIRD_PLACE_DEPTH_BONUS_THRESHOLD,
  THIRD_PLACE_DEPTH_PENALTY_THRESHOLD

New helper: _social_buckets_for_venue() maps a venue's types list to
the set of social-category buckets it belongs to. A venue with no
specific social type falls into "other" without inflating diversity.

Walk-time scoring logic unchanged. Headline venue selection unchanged.
Template rendering unchanged (score is still 0-10).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FcbHUqQcYoneuKh7Cr3ahN

* Add CLAUDE.md lessons from places scoring calibration PR

New patterns documented:
- Nullable field propagation audit checklist (arithmetic, formatting,
  templates, serialization)
- Venue scoring calibration patterns (eligibility thresholds, quality
  ceiling, suppressed dimensions)
- Decision log entries for quality ceiling and points=None strategy

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FcbHUqQcYoneuKh7Cr3ahN

---------

commit.message:
fix: E2E tests — RBAC for Polaris service proxy + settings selector (#22)

* fix: correct settings test selector to match plugin name

The settings E2E test looked for 'headlamp-polaris-plugin' but the
plugin is registered as 'polaris' (package.json name and
registerPluginSettings call). Fix the selector to match.

Refs: PRI-28

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: add RBAC manifest for Polaris dashboard service proxy access

E2E tests fail with 403 because users lack RBAC to proxy to the Polaris
dashboard service. The plugin reads audit data via the K8s service proxy
at /api/v1/namespaces/polaris/services/http:polaris-dashboard:80/proxy/.

Add deployment/polaris-rbac.yaml with:
- Role granting `get` on `services/proxy` for polaris-dashboard
- RoleBinding granting this to all authenticated users (read-only)

The E2E workflow also needs a `kubectl apply -f deployment/polaris-rbac.yaml`
step added before running tests. This requires the `workflows` permission
on the GitHub App, which is tracked separately.

Refs: PRI-28

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: add Polaris RBAC apply and readiness check to E2E workflow

The E2E tests fail because the CI runner lacks RBAC permissions to
proxy to the Polaris dashboard service. Apply the RBAC manifest
(added in this PR) and verify Polaris is reachable before running tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: remove kubectl steps from E2E workflow

The CI runner (local-ubuntu-latest) has no kubectl or cluster access.
E2E tests are browser-only via Playwright against a remote Headlamp URL.
The Polaris RBAC fix (deployment/polaris-rbac.yaml) must be applied
directly to the cluster by an operator with kubectl access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

batch tracking, and recall mechanism. Integrates with VibeDeviceNetwork.

feat: CLI node bootstrap + persistent NodeRegistry

- saveRegistry / loadRegistry: encode/decode NodeRegistry to/from disk using
  the existing ListNodes wire format (encodeListNodesResponse)
- newProtocolServer: loads registry from {dataDir}/node_registry.dat on startup
- handleBuiltinCluster: saves registry after every addNode / removeNode when
  dataDir is set

  supports --id, --host, --raft-port, --client-port, --data-dir, --join flags

  read the joining node's host from the parsed flags table

  20470-20479): round-trip, missing file, corrupt file, multi-cycle, field
  preservation — 6/6 passing
- Integration tests (tests/integration/test_bootstrap.nim, ports 20480-20489):
  join/remove persistence, registry reload via newProtocolServer, self-
  registration flow, no-dataDir behaviour — 6/6 passing
- Existing test_cluster_admin.nim: all 25 tests still passing

fix(security): remove localStorage token storage (M-13) (#104)

## Summary
- **M-13**: Remove all `localStorage` reads/writes for JWT token — auth
state is now driven by HttpOnly cookies set by the backend
- Frontend auth determined by `GET /api/auth/me` (cookie sent
automatically via `withCredentials: true`)
- `LoginView` no longer extracts `?token=` from URL — just calls
`fetchCurrentUser()` after OAuth redirect
- `profile.ts` calls `fetchCurrentUser()` instead of `updateToken()`
after role update
- All tests updated: `setToken()` → `setUser()`, `updateToken` mock →
`fetchCurrentUser` mock

Note: This depends on backend changes from Group 1 (PR #97) for the
HttpOnly cookie to be set.

## Test plan
- [ ] `npm run lint` passes
- [ ] `npm run test:unit` passes (294 tests)
- [ ] No `localStorage.getItem('mindtrack_token')` references remain
- [ ] Login flow works with cookie-based auth

Closes #91

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: address 4 bugs from testing feedback

1. Branch mismatch (#1): get_current_branch() now tries git
   symbolic-ref as fallback for fresh repos. Added resolve_dynasty_dir()
   to check main/master alternatives. All hooks use resolve_dynasty_dir.

2. Deterministic succession (#2): New core/succession.py replaces the
   LLM-driven subagent with pure Python. 8-step protocol runs without
   any AI judgment calls. 24 new tests including sacred Why: preservation.

3. Entry validation (#4): Added validate_entries() to core/entries.py.
   Warns on decisions missing Why: fields. Stop hook logs warnings to
   stderr.

4. Session counter (#8): Increment moved from Stop to SessionStart so
   crashed sessions still count toward succession pressure.

136 tests passing.

commit.message:
fix: half-capacity calculation in owner-aware seeding

Each bracket half needs bracket_size/2 meme slots, not bracket_size/4.
The bug caused seeding to fail for tournaments where meme count exceeded
bracket_size/4 (e.g. 38 memes in a 64-bracket).

- Show signal description at top of Summary tab
- Add Initiatives, Projects, Tasks, Goals as tag rows in Details tab
- Reduce spacing above Confidence drivers section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement hand-for-hand table pausing for tournament bubble play

ServerTableEngine gains pauseAfterHand()/resumeDealing() methods that
allow the tournament manager to hold tables between hands. When hand-
for-hand activates at the bubble, all table engines are paused after
their current hand completes. A 500ms sync interval checks when ALL
tables are waiting, then resumes them together for the next synchronized
hand. Includes 2-minute safety timeout per table in case of stalls.

On bubble burst, all engines permanently resume normal dealing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add apply command, correctness fixes, and documentation (#2)

* Add apply command with conflict detection and improved validation

Implement `named apply` to rename declarations and references directly
in source files based on validated report suggestions. Add cross-suggestion
conflict detection (G5) to prevent duplicate target names in the same scope,
constant naming convention enforcement, and text-based reference fallback
for references missed by the AST parser. Clean up banking-app samples to
remove semantic duplicates that caused rename collisions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add correctness fixes, per-file LLM batching, and safety guards

Correctness fixes (staff engineer review):
- B6/B7: Detect method override conflicts — blocks renames that would
  break subclass/interface implementations without propagation
- A3: Shadow collision detection — blocks field renames that shadow
  local variables in the same class
- A5: Import statement references — class renames now update import lines
- D1: Getter/setter mismatch warnings when field is renamed without
  updating paired accessors
- A4: String/comment exclusion in text-based reference fallback

Per-file LLM batching (6 API calls vs 322):
- One API call per Java file instead of one per symbol
- Full file source sent as context — LLM can see field types,
  siblings, and method bodies for better suggestions
- FileAnalysisPrompt renders numbered symbol list for indexed response

Safety guards for per-file batching:
- Dynamic max_tokens: len(symbols)*120+500, capped at 8000
- Chunking: files with >50 symbols split into chunks of 25
- finish_reason=length detection: warns and returns partial results
  gracefully instead of silently dropping symbols or crashing
- Hallucination filter: blocks method-style names (getX, setX, isX)
  suggested for fields or parameters

208 tests passing (28 new in test_llm_client.py)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add stakeholder report, technical report, and updated sample analysis

- STAKEHOLDER_REPORT.md: 10-slide factual report for stakeholder presentation
- TECHNICAL_REPORT.md: comprehensive technical documentation covering architecture,
  naming rules, guardrails, scoring, validation pipeline, and CLI usage
- docker_build.sh: Docker build helper script with dev/prod tagging strategy
- report/: updated sample analysis output from banking-app (127 suggestions, 95 valid)

commit.message:
docs: require merge verification before worktree cleanup (#38)

Agents were deleting worktrees after PR creation instead of after
merge confirmation. If the merge fails, the worktree — the only
working copy — is already gone, making retry impossible.

Changes:
- supervisor.md: Add step 3 "verify merge succeeded" between merge
  and cleanup, renumber subsequent steps
- .claude/worktrees/CLAUDE.md: Clarify cleanup happens only after
  PR is merged, add gh pr view verification check

commit.message:
Fix terminal not filling modal: wait for xterm first render before fit (#28)

fitAddon.fit() called immediately after term.open() fails because xterm
hasn't measured charWidth/charHeight yet (they're 0 until the first render).
Use term.onRender() to defer fit until xterm's render service is initialized.

commit.message:
perf: replace GenServer.cast with plain send for write path

Drop the $gen_cast tuple wrapper on all write messages to series
actors. Writes now go through handle_info instead of handle_cast,
saving one tuple allocation per message. Benchmarked at ~17% lower
isolated send cost (9.7ms → 8.0ms per 10K sends) and ~7.6% higher
individual write throughput (901K → 970K pts/sec).

feat: Agent Protocol + RWA Layer + Real Estate — VSOS absorbs AI agents and physical assets

AGENTS (absorbing Paperclip, Pippin, Google GenAI):
- VibeAgentProtocol: Universal AI agent infrastructure with multi-framework
  compatibility (VSOS/Paperclip/Pippin/Google/OpenAI/Anthropic), autonomy
  levels (supervised→fully autonomous), CRPC messaging, Proof of Mind
  scoring, skill registry, and Shapley attribution.
- VibeAgentMarketplace: Agent discovery and hiring with task management,
  ratings, revenue sharing (95/5 split), and capability matching.
- VibeAgentOrchestrator: Multi-agent workflows with DAG-based step
  execution, agent swarms with consensus (majority/unanimous/weighted).
- VibeAgentMemory: Persistent verifiable memory with episodic/semantic/
  procedural/contextual types, shared memory spaces, memory graph linking.

RWA (Real World Assets):
- VibeRWA: Universal asset tokenization — real estate, commodities, art,
  vehicles, IP, revenue streams. Fractional ownership with yield distribution,
  secondary market, and oracle-verified appraisals.
- VibeRealEstate: DECENTRALIZED ZILLOW — P2P property transactions with
  smart contract escrow, fractional ownership, rental income distribution,
  staked appraisal network. No realtors. No closing costs. No banks.

commit.message:
docs: initial Mintlify documentation — guides, API reference, conventions (#1033)

* docs: initial Mintlify docs — guides, API reference, and conventions

Full documentation covering the Vertz stack:
- Getting started: quickstart, installation, conventions
- UI guides: components, reactivity, styling, routing, data fetching, forms, SSR, compiler
- Server guides: entities, services (renamed from actions), overview
- DB guides: schema (FK from relations, index types, model-level tenancy), queries, migrations
- Cross-cutting: errors-as-values, schema validation, SDK & fetch client
- API reference: mount() (no selector), SSR handler, compiler plugin stubs
- Updated for recent PRs: invalidate(), auto-revalidation, vertz start, vertz/ui-server subpath

  (watch doesn't exist, signal shouldn't be public, QueryResult types
  hide .value via Unwrapped<ReadonlySignal<T>>)

* docs: fix refetch() comment — does not clear cache [#1033]

refetch() re-fetches in the background and updates the cache when new
data arrives. It does not clear the cache first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: clarify invalidate() matches by entity type, not cache key [#1033]

invalidate(api.todos.list()) revalidates ALL active list queries for
that entity type, including filtered ones. Matching is by entity type
+ operation kind, not by cache key or query params.

repository.name: vertz
repository.description: The TypeScript stack for LLMs

commit.message:
Add global entry type caps per competition event

Admins can now set a maximum number of entries per entry type (Solo,
Duo, Trio, etc.) across ALL studios for a given event — separate from
the existing per-registration limit.

- models/schemas: `entry_type_global_limits` TEXT column on events
  (JSON: {"Solo": 50, "Duo": 30})
- migration: idempotent ALTER TABLE in run_migrations()
- _build_event_out: exposes the new field in EventOut
- create entry endpoint: enforces global cap with a cross-registration
  COUNT query; returns 400 with a descriptive message when full
- admin.html: "Global Entry Caps" section below "Entry Limits per
  Registration" in the Competition Settings panel; inputs labelled
  "entries max / event"; wired into saveEvent(), openEditEvent(), and
  togglePricingSection()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
